_about: Second 20-PR eval set for the litellm-bot PR-review agent. Mix of provider fixes, integrations, docs-only, deps-shaped diffs, and CI-failure shapes (lint, code-quality, CodeQL, GitGuardian, branch-meta). Each entry carries a `human_label` (ready/not_ready/null) plus a short `human_notes` capturing what the manual grader saw at sample time. Edit notes when adding new entries.

_label_legend.ready: Human grader judged the PR ready for review (would map to agent verdict READY).
_label_legend.not_ready: Human grader judged the PR NOT ready for review (would map to agent verdict BLOCKED, or WAITING when the only blocker is in-progress checks).
_label_legend.null: Not yet graded by a human — agent verdict shown but no agree/disagree contribution to the matrix.

prs.notes: Fix qdrant semantic cache. Greptile 5/5. All 44 checks green. No proof.
prs.human_notes: Greptile 5/5, small targeted fix, well-explained problem, low Veria warning.

prs.notes: fix(content_filter): log guardrail_information on streaming. Greptile 5/5. All 71 checks green.
prs.human_notes: Passing all testing, Greptile 5/5, low Veria. Would urge author to share a screenshot/proof of fix — that is missing.

prs.notes: fix(proxy): set verbose_logger level when LITELLM_LOG=INFO. Greptile 5/5. Only failing check is meta (Verify PR source branch). Note: duplicate of #26429.
prs.human_notes: Greptile 5/5, small targeted fix, no Veria warning. Meta check failing isn't a cause for concern (whitelist this CI check as unrelated).

prs.notes: fix(anthropic): drop temperature from reasoning params. Greptile 4/5. Failing: CodeQL, All Other Providers, branch-meta.
prs.human_notes: Failing tests which may be related to this change.

prs.notes: feat(vertex_ai): Model Garden OpenAPI for publisher model ids. Greptile 4/5. All 72 checks green. Proof attached.
prs.human_notes: The heuristic concerns raised by Greptile are real. There are serious code quality issues in this PR.

prs.notes: Large stale PR. 77 file changes, multiple merge conflicts.
prs.human_notes: 77 file changes — too large. Too many merge conflicts. Clearly a stale PR and needs to be redone.

prs.notes: Fix sets error_msg to None to suppress an error. Issue is real but the chosen approach masks the failure surface.
prs.human_notes: The approach is wrong. The issue is real, but the fix to make error_msg None would mean users can no longer understand why their request failed. Fails the principle of fixing bugs at the source, not at the sink.

prs.notes: Lint url encodings. Touches 66 files applying an encoding helper inline at every callsite.
prs.human_notes: Approach is brittle and prone to failure. Changes 66 files which is too much. Encoding of a particular var inline across all these files means that in 3 months, if someone adds a new file with a similar URL/encoding and forgets the helper, we're back to square one.

prs.notes: Deletes a set of imports and hides them behind a feature flag.
prs.human_notes: Dangerous PR. Deletes a bunch of imports and hides them behind a feature flag, but for existing proxy users this would mean those routes now just fail. Extremely dangerous — would lead to production down.
